faq.title: Questions sponsors ask
faq.q1: Will I see the student's identity?
faq.a1: No. To protect everyone, you see an anonymous profile only — never a name, IC, address, phone, email or photo. The student never sees your details either.
faq.a2: Funds are held by Yayasan myNADI and released against verified study milestones. Money is never handed directly to a student.
faq.q3: Can I choose who I support?
faq.a3: Yes. Once approved, you can browse the anonymised profiles and choose the student you'd like to support.
faq.q4: What happens if a student withdraws?
faq.a4: If a student leaves their studies, any remaining funds stay within the programme to support another verified student.
faq.q5: How much does it cost to sponsor someone?
faq.a5: It depends on the student's programme and needs. Each profile shows the support required so you can decide what works for you.
faq.a6: HalaTuju runs the B40 Assistance Programme together with Yayasan myNADI, which holds and disburses the funds.

help.income: If your family is an STR recipient, please upload a screenshot of your STR portal showing your parent's name and NRIC. We strongly encourage you to also upload the latest salary slip and/or EPF statement for every working member of the household.

help.guardianship_letter: A photo or PDF of a court-issued guardianship order or a written authorisation letter from your parent, showing that you are under their care.

fallback.name_mismatch: The name we read on this doesn't match the name in your profile. Either the photo is unclear, or the name you typed when registering is spelled differently. Upload a clearer photo if that's the cause; if the card is correct, fix the spelling in your profile.
fallback.nric_mismatch: The IC number we read doesn't match the one in your profile. Check the digits in your profile, then upload a clear photo of your IC again.
fallback.ic_nric_misread: The name on your IC matched, but the IC number didn't match the one on your account — usually a misread of the digits. Upload a clear, straight-on photo with no glare on the number. If it still differs, check the IC number in your profile.
fallback.address_mismatch: We couldn't find your home address on this bill. The bill confirms where you live, so make sure the address is clearly visible on the page you upload, then try again.
fallback.wrong_doc: This doesn't look like the document expected here. Check you picked the right file, then upload again.
fallback.unreadable: We couldn't read this clearly — it may be blurry or dark. Upload a clearer, well-lit photo or a fresh scan.
fallback.review_manually: We couldn't auto-check this one, so a reviewer will look at it by hand. There's nothing more you need to do here.
fallback.slip_name_mismatch: The name on this results slip isn't yours — it looks like someone else's slip. Upload your own results slip; the name on it must match yours.
fallback.slip_subjects_missing: Your results slip lists a subject you haven't added to your profile. Add the missing subject(s) so they match the slip — or upload the right file if this one was a mix-up.
fallback.slip_grade_mismatch: A grade on your slip differs from what you entered. The slip is the official record, so update the grade in your profile to match it. If the photo is blurry, upload a clearer one instead.
fallback.slip_grade_uncertain: We read your slip but couldn't be fully sure of one grade. Check that the grades you entered match the slip; if one differs, correct it in your profile. A clearer, straight-on photo helps too.
fallback.slip_skewed_unclear: Your slip came through at an angle, which left one part hard to read. Lay it flat, fill the frame, and photograph it straight from above, then upload again.
fallback.offer_name_mismatch: The name or IC number on this offer letter isn't yours — it looks like someone else's letter. Upload your own offer letter; the name and IC on it must match yours.
fallback.offer_pathway_mismatch: The college or programme on this offer letter differs from the study choice you entered earlier. That's fine — plans change, and it doesn't block anything. You can update your pathway in your Profile to match this offer, or leave it and we'll ask you to confirm which one is final after you submit.
fallback.income_relationship_mismatch: The name on this IC doesn't link to your family the way we expected — for a parent or sibling we look for your father's name from your own IC; for a mother, your birth certificate. So either a different person's MyKad was uploaded here, or (for a mother) the birth certificate names someone else. Please check you uploaded the right MyKad for this person and, for a mother, your own birth certificate (showing you and her). Your application isn't blocked.
fallback.income_proof_person_mismatch: This salary slip / EPF isn't for the same person as the IC for that family member — it looks like someone else's document. If you're already showing your family's income another way (your STR), you don't need a salary slip here: just remove this file, and if that family member has none, don't upload one. Otherwise, upload that family member's own salary slip with their own IC, and remove the wrong file. Nothing is blocked.
fallback.income_proof_needed: The earner's IC is in and matches. The next step is their salary slip — upload that family member's latest salary slip, which shows the household income (any relationship document, like a birth certificate, comes after). Nothing is blocked.
fallback.income_ic_needed: You've uploaded an income document for this family member, but not that person's MyKad yet. The IC is required — please add it to complete your application, so we can confirm the income document belongs to them.
fallback.income_rel_doc_needed: The earner's IC is in and matches the income document. The last required step is the document that links them to you — a birth certificate for a mother, or a guardianship letter for a guardian. Please upload it to complete your application.
fallback.income_rel_doc_unreadable: You've uploaded your birth certificate (or guardianship letter), but we couldn't read the names on it that link the earner to you — it may be unclear, or the wrong document. Please check it's a clear photo or PDF of the actual document and upload it again to complete your application.
fallback.str_not_current: We couldn't confirm this STR shows it was approved. To prove your family qualifies, upload your STR showing “Lulus” — a MySTR “Semakan Status” or Dashboard screenshot showing the status “Lulus” (with your parent's name and IC), or your official STR approval letter. A plain screenshot that shows “Lulus” is enough. Please make sure it's your STR (Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah) — a SARA letter is a different aid and isn't accepted on its own. No current STR? You can show your family's income with a salary slip / EPF instead.
fallback.generic: Something didn't look right with this upload. Have another look, then try uploading again.

hero.heading: A community behind every student.
hero.sub: A community-supported pilot helping students from low-income households continue their studies at Malaysia's public colleges and universities — from pre-university through to degree — backed by fellow Malaysians who want to see them succeed.

overview.p1: Every year, promising students from low-income households earn a place at Malaysian public institutions — to pursue Pre-university studies, Certificate, Diploma and Degree — only to find they lack the means to make the most of it. This programme exists to close that gap.
overview.p2: The model is straightforward. Members of the community choose to support a student through their studies. It is assistance, not a loan — there is nothing to repay. Our hope is that those who are supported today will, in time, support others tomorrow.

card1.title: Realise your potential
card1.desc: Practical support to help you make the most of your place at a public institution.

card2.title: A community behind you
card2.desc: Funded by individuals, not a faceless institution, who want you to succeed.

card3.title: Your privacy, your control
card3.desc: Sponsors only see what you choose to share.

note.title: Please note
note.pilotText: Expect some uncertainty as we get going. We'll be transparent about what we don't know yet.
note.noGuaranteeText: Applying lets us assess your eligibility. It does not guarantee assistance.
note.limitedText: We try to support every eligible student, but places in this pilot are limited.
note.accurateText: Honest answers help us match you to opportunities you're genuinely eligible for, and protect places for those who need them.
note.verifyText: We take your application in good faith. We may ask for supporting documents to confirm key details.
note.under18Text: A parent or guardian must give consent on your behalf before your profile is shared with any sponsor.
note.confidentialText: Your answers are shared only with the programme coordinators, the administering NGO, and any sponsor you specifically consent to.

req.title: Can I apply?
req.item1: Malaysian citizen who has just completed, or is completing, SPM or STPM.
req.item2: From a low-income household — combined monthly income at or below RM5,860, the national B40 threshold (DOSM, 2024).
req.item3: Solid academic record — at least 5 A's in SPM, or a PNGK of 3.0 or above in STPM.
req.item4: Continuing at a Malaysian public institution — Asasi, Matrikulasi, STPM, Certificate, Diploma or Degree.
req.item5: Reachable for a short interview — usually a 20-minute phone call in English, Bahasa Malaysia, or Tamil.
req.item6: Willing to share progress — semester results and a brief update, so that support can continue through your studies.
req.closeNote: Close, but not exactly? Apply anyway. Our bar is a little more generous than the headline above — especially on grades — so it's worth applying even if you don't match every line exactly. Applying lets us assess your eligibility; it doesn't guarantee assistance.

how.title: How it works
how.rolling: No fixed deadline — reviewed on a rolling basis
how.step1Desc: Sign in with Google, confirm your HalaTuju profile, and submit. About 5–10 minutes.
how.step2Desc: You'll receive an email acknowledging your application on the day you submit it.
how.step3Desc: We check your results and household income against the criteria and let you know whether you've been shortlisted.
how.step4Desc: If shortlisted, we'll ask you to share a few more details and upload supporting documents (IC, results slip, proof of household income, etc.). We'll tell you exactly what's needed.
how.step5Desc: A brief phone call, usually 20 minutes, in your preferred language. This is to get to know you, not to test you.
how.step6Desc: With your written consent, we prepare a confidential profile for sponsors to consider.
how.step7Desc: Once a sponsor chooses to support you, Yayasan myNADI arranges the assistance and administers the funds. From application to award typically takes up to two months. (Partnership being finalised.)
how.step8Desc: Each semester, you upload your results and a short progress note. As long as you're progressing, the support continues.

faq.title: Questions
faq.q1: Do I need to have used HalaTuju before?
faq.a1: No. If you're new, the same Google sign-in creates your account. If you've already done the HalaTuju course-matching quiz, we'll use what's in your profile so you don't have to retype anything.
faq.q2: Is this a loan? Do I have to pay it back?
faq.a2: No. It is education assistance from a sponsor — nothing to repay, no interest. Our hope is that those who are supported today will, in time, support others tomorrow.
faq.q3: Is the assistance guaranteed if I qualify?
faq.a3: No. This is a pilot with limited places and strict criteria. Applying lets us assess you; it does not guarantee assistance.
faq.q4: Who can see my personal details?
faq.a4: Your private details — your IC, contact details, and home address — are never shown to sponsors. Sponsors only see a profile, and only after you have given your consent. If you are under 18, a parent or guardian must give that consent on your behalf.
faq.a5: Only after you've been shortlisted: your IC, your SPM or STPM results slip, and proof of household income or aid (such as your STR letter, an EPF statement, or a payslip). We'll tell you exactly what's needed at that stage — there is nothing to upload when you first apply.
faq.a6: Public post-secondary study only — both pre-university (Asasi at a university or Polytechnic, Matrikulasi, or STPM) and tertiary (at a University, Polytechnic, Community College, IPGM, ILJTM or ILKBS).
faq.a7: No. The programme supports study at public institutions only. Foundation or Asasi programmes must also be at a public institution (IPTA) to qualify.
faq.a8: Not at present — applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. You'll receive an acknowledgement on the same day you apply, and we aim to confirm shortlisting within 48 hours. From application to being matched with a sponsor typically takes up to two months.
faq.q9: Is there a cost to apply?
faq.a9: No. Applying is free and takes about 5–10 minutes.

donor.title: Want to support a student?
donor.body: This programme exists because individuals in the community choose to fund the studies of specific students. If you'd like to know more about supporting a student — whether for a semester, a year, or the full course of their studies — we'd be glad to hear from you.

about.title: About this programme
about.body: The B40 Assistance Programme is coordinated by HalaTuju, with Yayasan myNADI expected to administer the funds (partnership being finalised), and a network of community organisations — including Sri Murugan Centre (SMC), Concerned Universiti Malaya Indian Graduates (CUMIG) and others — that refer and support students.

about.problemP1: Every year, thousands of Malaysian students leave school and enrol in expensive private colleges — often taking on heavy student loans for qualifications that don't always match what's available for free or at heavily subsidised rates in the public system.
about.problemP2: In 2024, over 137,000 places were available across polytechnics, community colleges, TVET institutions, and public universities — not including UiTM. These are government-funded, industry-recognised programmes. But too many students and families never find them, because the information is scattered and hard to navigate.
about.whatDesc: Enter your SPM or STPM results, and our eligibility engine checks them against the entry requirements of 1,300+ courses across 800+ public institutions in Malaysia. We help you find the programmes you're actually eligible for — matched to your grades, your interests, and your goals.
about.whoP1: HalaTuju is built under Project Lentera, a community-driven initiative focused on improving access to public tertiary education for Malaysian students.
about.whoP2: This is a volunteer effort — no single person owns it. Teachers, counsellors, parents, alumni, and community organisations like Yayasan myNADI and Concerned UM Indian Graduates (CUMIG) all play a part. We believe every student deserves to make an informed choice, not a default one driven by lack of information.
about.helpParentsDesc: Use HalaTuju with your child after SPM or STPM results. The options may surprise you.
about.helpTeachersDesc: Share it with your students. Five minutes could save a family years of debt.
about.helpEveryoneDesc: Spread the word. The more families who know about public options, the fewer who choose private ones simply because they didn't know better.
about.helpClosing: HalaTuju is a growing platform — we're continuously adding courses and improving recommendations. If you have feedback or want to contribute, we want to hear from you.

matric-sains.headline: Fast Track to University for Science Students
matric-sains.description: A 1–2 year matriculation programme under KPM that prepares you for a bachelor's degree in science, medicine, pharmacy, and related fields. You study Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology at pre-university level — more rigorous than SPM but with lecturer support and tutorials. Admission is based on SPM merit and quota allocation.

matric-kejuruteraan.headline: A Strong Foundation for an Engineering Career
matric-kejuruteraan.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme focused on Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry — the three pillars of engineering. You get early exposure to engineering concepts before progressing to a bachelor's degree at a public university. Ideal for students who enjoy solving technical problems and building things.

matric-sains-komputer.headline: Start Your Career in Information Technology
matric-sains-komputer.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme covering programming fundamentals, discrete mathematics, and introduction to computer systems. Designed for students who want to pursue a degree in computer science, software engineering, or information technology at a public university.

matric-perakaunan.headline: Your First Step into Finance and Accounting
matric-perakaunan.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme providing foundations in accounting principles, economics, and business mathematics. Prepares you for a bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or business management at a public university. Suited for students who are detail-oriented with numbers and interested in the business world.

stpm-sains.headline: An Internationally Recognised Pre-U Qualification
stpm-sains.description: STPM Science is an 18-month programme (3 terms) at Form 6, equivalent to A-Levels. You choose 3–4 science subjects (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology) and sit examinations each term. STPM results are accepted by public and private universities locally and internationally. The best option if you want an open pathway to a wide range of universities.

stpm-sains-sosial.headline: A Flexible Pathway to Arts, Law, and Business
stpm-sains-sosial.description: STPM Social Science is an 18-month programme (3 terms) at Form 6 covering subjects like Economics, Business Studies, History, Geography, and Literature. An A-Level equivalent qualification that opens doors to degrees in law, public administration, communications, and more. Suited for students who enjoy analysing social issues and thinking critically.

utility_holder_unknown.fact: A utility bill is in another person's name: {name} — neither the student nor an uploaded parent.

utility_address_mismatch.fact: A utility bill's supply address differs from the home address on file.
utility_address_mismatch.question: The address on the utility bill looks different from the stated home address — which is the current home, and whose bill is the other one?

payslip_epf_divergence.fact: A working member's payslip (RM {slip}) and EPF-implied salary (RM {epf_implied}) differ noticeably.
payslip_epf_divergence.question: The payslip and EPF figures don't line up — is there overtime or commission, or were contributions paid late? What is the regular monthly pay?

ic_low_confidence.fact: The identity card doesn't look like a photo of a physical MyKad (likely typed text, a printout, or a screenshot) — the name and IC number may still match what was entered.
ic_low_confidence.question: The IC image doesn't look like a genuine card — confirm the applicant's physical MyKad at interview.

parent_ic_low_confidence.fact: The parent/guardian's identity card doesn't look like a photo of a physical MyKad (likely typed text, a printout, or a screenshot).
parent_ic_low_confidence.question: The parent/guardian IC image doesn't look like a genuine card — confirm the physical card at interview.

document_not_genuine.fact: The uploaded {doc} doesn't look like a genuine official document — it appears to be: {seen}.

funding_other_without_note.fact: Student ticked “Other” under funding but left the note blank.

declaration_name_mismatch.fact: Signed declaration name “{declaration}” differs from the profile name “{profile_name}”.
declaration_name_mismatch.question: The name you signed differs from your profile — small typo, or are these genuinely different names?

device_in_funding.question: RM 3,000 may not cover a laptop alongside other costs. If we can only support partially, how would you bridge the gap?

verdict.title: Verification verdict
verdict.intro: What the checks confirm, and what still needs you. Audit it — don't rebuild it.

item.ic_low_confidence: The identity card may not be a genuine photo of a physical MyKad — confirm the card at interview.
item.document_not_genuine: This document may not be a genuine official original — confirm it at interview.
item.name_resolved_truncation: Name confirmed via NRIC — the IC's printed name was read short (“{ocr_name}”), a shortened form of the name on file “{profile_name}”.
item.results_slip_name_ok: The results slip is in the student's name.
item.str_verified: STR document verified — B40 status confirmed.
item.offer_name_ok: Offer letter is in the student's name.
item.ic_unreadable: The identity card could not be read — ask for a clearer photo.
item.ic_service_down: The ID-reading service was unavailable — re-check shortly.
item.nric_mismatch: The IC reads NRIC {ocr_nric}; the student typed {profile_nric}. Confirm.
item.name_mismatch: The IC name “{ocr_name}” differs from the typed name “{profile_name}”. Confirm.
item.address_state_mismatch: The IC is registered in {ic_state}; the student says {profile_state}. Ask which is current.
item.results_slip_name_mismatch: The results slip is in a different name. Confirm.
item.results_slip_unreadable: The results slip could not be read — ask for a clearer copy.
item.grades_unverified: Grades not yet cross-checked against the slip — eyeball them (automated check coming).
item.str_present_unverified: STR document present but not yet matched to the household — confirm.
item.income_proof_missing: No STR document uploaded — this household is on the STR route, so request the STR (a salary slip or EPF won't substitute).
item.income_earner_undeclared: The student hasn't said whose income they're showing (the income wizard is incomplete) — we can't check the documents until they do.
item.earner_ic_missing: No IC uploaded for: {members} — we can't confirm the income documents belong to the family. Request it.
item.earner_ic_unreadable: Couldn't read the IC for: {members} — ask for a clearer photo.
item.birth_cert_missing: A working member is the mother, but no birth certificate links her to the student. Request it.
item.birth_cert_mismatch: The birth certificate doesn't link the named mother to the student — check by eye.
item.father_patronymic_mismatch: The IC name for {members} doesn't match the father's name in the student's own IC — check the relationship.
item.guardianship_letter_missing: Income shown is a guardian's, but no guardianship letter/order was uploaded. Request it.
item.str_not_current: The STR doesn't positively show it was approved — it may be an application record (SALINAN) with no status, an out-of-date (prior-year) STR, or one we couldn't read. Ask for the MySTR “Lulus” status (Semakan Status / Dashboard) or the approval letter; or accept the salary route.
item.str_recipient_mismatch: The STR recipient isn't the declared earner — the name/IC on the STR doesn't match the earner's IC. Confirm.
item.income_unverified_needs_interview: Income can't be document-verified (informal / no payslip) — confirm during the interview via household size, dependents and lifestyle. Do NOT block.
item.income_per_capita_ok: Per-capita income RM{amount} is below the B40 line (RM{ceiling}) — within the threshold.
item.income_above_b40_line: Per-capita income RM{amount} is at/above the B40 line (RM{ceiling}) — the documents don't confirm B40 need. A human must decide at interview (circumstances may still apply). Do NOT auto-reject.
item.utility_percapita_b40: Utility proxy: combined water + electricity ~RM{amount}/capita/month — consistent with a B40 household (soft, imperfect signal).
item.utility_percapita_high: Utility proxy: combined water + electricity ~RM{amount}/capita/month — high (M40/T20 consumption pattern). Worth probing at interview (soft signal).
item.utility_hardship: Hardship signal: the utility bills carry meaningful arrears (unpaid balance) — supports financial need.
item.relationship_confirmed: Earner confirmed as the student's parent/guardian.
item.offer_name_mismatch: The offer letter is in a different name or IC. Confirm.
item.offer_unreadable: The offer letter could not be read — ask for a clearer copy.
item.offer_no_identity: This letter has no student name or IC on it — it looks like a general notice, not the personal offer letter. Ask for the actual offer letter addressed to the student.
item.pathway_confirm: Offer for {programme} at {institution} differs from the declared pathway — awaiting the student's confirmation of which is final.
item.pathway_confirmed: Final pathway confirmed by the student: {programme} at {institution}.
item.academic_missing_subjects: Entered {entered} of {total} subjects on the slip — missing: {subjects}. Ask the student to add them.
item.academic_grade_mismatch: {subject}: typed {typed}, but the slip reads {slip}. Check.
item.academic_grade_uncertain: {subject}: our read shows {slip}, but the student typed {typed} — an A/A+ (±) read we can't be sure of. Check it by eye against the slip.
item.grades_verified: All {count} grades match the results slip.
item.offer_letter_missing: No offer letter uploaded — required before we can support a place.
item.course_unspecified: No course/field chosen yet — the student has been asked what they plan to study.
item.sibling_level_unknown: Sibling school-vs-tertiary split not captured — the student has been asked to split the count.
item.device_status_unknown: Device need unknown — the student has been asked whether they have a laptop/tablet.
item.transport_cost_unknown: Transport cost unknown — the student has been asked how they travel and the rough monthly cost.
item.utility_holder_unknown: Utility bill is in another person's name — the student has been asked who they are and the relationship.
item.utility_address_mismatch: Utility-bill address differs from the home address — the student has been asked which is their current home.
